ai_disclosure: Grounded in real public data (weather via Open-Meteo, public holidays via Nager.Date) where available, phrased by AI (Gemini) and AI copy-reviewed against the saudade editorial rules. Written for this edition's readers — not translated from English. No human rewrite.

items.headline: Persistent rain marks the Gwanghwamun square this week.
items.lede: Expect 23.4mm of precipitation as the city cools to a range of 22–25°C.
items.body: The atmosphere remains heavy with moisture as steady rain persists across the capital. With temperatures holding between 22–25°C, the air feels damp rather than oppressive. You might seek shelter near Gwanghwamun Square, where the Seoul Summer Beach installation provides a temporary water park for residents. The exchange rate currently sits at 1 USD to 1485 KRW, making local amenities accessible for visitors. As the sun sets at 19:50, the city lights begin to reflect on the wet pavement. Keep an umbrella close as you navigate the moderate air quality, with PM2.5 levels measured at 46.
